old_claim.note: Public Archives provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct public_archives edge remains because preserved written route guides such as the Periplus are merchant or travel texts, not technologies that require a public archival repository.

source_shape.source_locator: Introduction and sections 1-43: identifies the Periplus as a mid-first-century CE Greek merchant text and gives sailing routes, distances, anchorages, hazards, market towns, and sailing seasons.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source supports a route-guide text for merchants and navigators, not a route-guide technology dependent on public archive institutions.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the corrected node is about written route guidance, the material source requirement is writing and route/navigation knowledge; public archives may preserve examples but do not enable their creation.

ontology_before: Itineraries & Route Guides depended directly on public_archives, implying that archival repositories enabled the route-guide form.
ontology_after: Itineraries & Route Guides are scoped to written route and sailing guides; public archives are excluded as preservation context rather than a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: itineraries_route_guides no longer directly depends on public_archives.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to official archival road registers rather than route guides used by travelers and traders.
A source showed the earliest scoped route guides were created as public archive instruments rather than as travel or merchant texts.
The public_archives node were broadened to include all written documentary media rather than institutional archives.
short_reason: Route guides can be written and used without a public archive institution.
